author: @giovannip.1433
text: What would be perfect would be an old, unused exercycle with a comfy seat stored in the storm shelter - have that hooked up to your generator. At least you can take turns pedaling while relatively safe and secure and not really get tired.

author: @suzannehartmann946
text: We absolutely fill the bath tub with water here in the southern USA where we get several hurricanes a year before they come in.  We have "hurricane lamps" have to remember to get the fuel for them and keep them up high so they don't get knocked over.  Or of course BIG lanterns that eat through large batteries.  We do have hand cranked radios for sale here from and ton of sources that include of course the weather /emergency station "dialed in".  And usually have small coolers that we fill with ice to keep some perishables in before the storm because a fridge is a large space.  The cooler is portable if we have to evacuate due to flooding.  Preparedness is something we live with here.  Did you see what happened in Waverly Tennessee here?  That type of flash flood is unusual here in the eastern seaboard.  But it is common in the southwest in AZ where i used to live.  I am sick of disasters.  I have had my stuff destroyed in too many of them.  I am tired of starting over.  The military and moving was like one long disaster.  This is my 34th address.  Please pray for ALL of the people affected by the disaster in Afghanistan.  I cannot even fathom the panic the Afghan women are facing from dealing with those sociopaths the Taliban men.  I live next door to Ft. Bragg we have men over there right now.  Thank you for the upbeat video.

author: @Kiyarose3999
text: Oh no, you’ve gone and bought unnecessary ‘’Stuff’’, including another blender, you recently bought 1 to show us how the motor can be used as a generator. Why couldn’t you save the resources, and use the previous blender motor to demonstrate with?. Considering the world is falling apart, we really shouldn’t be buying unnecessary Stuff!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: people don't watch all the videos mate - if I did as you say I can guarantee questions on where to get the blender

replies.author: @ashyslashy5818
replies.text: so u gonna save the world from not buying a blender.i guess ive totally destroyed the planet then.i buy disposable razors and hold on to ur hat SOAP...

replies.author: @Kiyarose3999
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Why, did you have people asking where you got the first blender?, still don’t see any good reason why u  used another new blender to demonstrate. If u think people would ask where you got it from, just tell them, as you often do.

replies.author: @Kiyarose3999
replies.text: @ashyslashy5818 As we know, it’s not just one blender, but 100’s of millions of them, the world IS falling apart so to me buying unnecessary ’’Stuff’’ is unconscionable. That has parts from all around the world, a plastic casing that adds to the piles of plastic waste, and electronics adding to the vast ewaste problem (usually dumped in poor countries) We need to look and the wider picture, from Mine to ewaste dump!

author: @docink6175
text: I live in Florida so I have a lot of hurricane preparedness both in the house and my van is set up to support me for 2-3 wks, I like this hand gennie idea, maybe to recharge a battery bank? can I just cut a usb cable or is it best to use the plug you showed? Water in the tub is ok but you end up either scooping up the last bit with a cup or wasting it, store that same water in gallon jugs and use smaller bottles to fill the gaps.. I say gallon jugs because they are easier for us older folk to lift and pour from than 5gallon and 1 gallon will usually flush a toilet

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: the plug has voltage stabilisation built in so it will work from around 3 volts up to 12 volts as input and will output a steady charge voltage suitable for phones etc - so I would say best to use the plug as a hand gennie is going to be an unstable voltage

author: @headlessxp4948
text: I thought I would share an video idea regarding the same topic: making a mechanical buck converter regulator to charge your phone from your car. And since you would need this in an power outage situation, soldering iron, power tools & multimeters are not allowed. That means the voltage calibration part would be quite a challenge.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yess it would - hence the car charger plug!

author: @deslomeslager
text:
In the near future you will just take power from either your e-bike or e-car.
Do we still need a radio at hand? It is getting outdated. More and more radio stations are no longer doing the electrical (antenna) broad casting on FM and are switching to DAB(+) or internet. But DAB+ in my region has bad repentance, so I am not sure how to 'listen' to anything. Luckily I do have HAM radios and 27MC radios at hand. For just in case :-) (and they are charged and checked regularly). And charged power banks all over the place, using 18650 old laptop batteries (8 per power bank).
Do I need all of this? No. Not here, in the Netherlands. Let's call it a hobby then.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: to me - our world is ridiculously fragile and our dependance and belief in it equally worrying - ah well

author: @stevetobias4890
text: It's actually amazing what you can make out of bits and pieces from different items. Love the McGuiver comment because that show is a classic example of how you can get out of a fix with some out of the box thinking.

author: @jasonhaymanonthedrawingboard
text: If you put a resistance on the out flow of a capacitor you can control the discharge of current. It means solid state batteries are easy enough to make. A simple rheostat employed correctly will give variability and the ability to dial things in. As your taking the layered approach .it should be easy enough to make a capacitor box with suitable quantities. it will be enough to power a house. In storm condition raise shields phrase for sci-fi could even become possible. 1000 year batteries and soo much more. Nice work keep it up! Fast changing slow releases is alway the best way to go. Might even recommend a fly back circuit with appropriate reset fuses leading to the resistor. Should things decide to overload their won’t be a huge explosion. Or a mess to clean up.
